title: POST-H-019-B — Permission model y manifest hardening
commands: python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_permission_model.py tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_sandbox_design.py tests/test_plugin_registry.py tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_project_global_state.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_sandbox_policy.py -q
risks: Confundir un permiso declarado como autorización de ejecución.
Permitir permisos desconocidos por accidente en manifests de plugins.
Relajar dynamic_import/subprocess/network/filesystem_write antes de una ADR futura.
limitations: POST-H-019-B valida permisos y manifests; no implementa install dry-run ni exposure report.
Plugin execution, importlib, subprocess, red, filesystem write, pip install y marketplace permanecen bloqueados.
Quality gate plugin-sandbox-design queda para POST-H-019-D y runbook final para POST-H-019-E.
notes: Plugin execution remains blocked by design.
Any future executable plugin capability requires ADR, sandbox, RBAC, approvals, tests and rollback.
FIX-01: components package must include tests/test_post_h_019_plugin_sandbox_design.py because POST-H-019-B advances current_micro_sprint from A to B.
pass_criteria: Threat model covers at least 10 plugin threats.
No-go gates are documented for importlib, subprocess, network, filesystem write, pip install and marketplace.
Documents have approved frontmatter and source registry/TCR synchronization.
Plugin registry validation remains metadata-only and non-executable.
block_criteria: BLOCK if the design enables plugin execution.
BLOCK if dynamic import, subprocess, shell, network, external API or pip install are proposed as current capabilities.
BLOCK if manifests are treated as execution permission.
BLOCK if threats for secrets, red or filesystem are omitted.
